message:
fix(extensions-js): decouple __dirname and __filename CJS shim injection and improve __dirname derivation

CJS-to-ESM transpiler fixes:

- Split __dirname/__filename detection into separate boolean flags
  (has_dirname_refs, has_filename_refs) instead of combining them,
  so that source files using only __dirname do not unnecessarily
  receive a __filename shim, and vice versa
- Fix needs_filename to check has_filename_refs (was incorrectly
  checking has_dirname_refs, causing __filename shims to be injected
  when only __dirname was referenced)
- Improve __dirname shim to derive the directory from import.meta.url
  via URL().pathname instead of depending on a __filename binding;
  this avoids the prior circular dependency where __dirname required
  __filename which required import.meta.url anyway
- Wrap __dirname derivation in a try/catch IIFE with '.' fallback for
  environments where import.meta.url may not resolve to a file path

New tests:
- maybe_cjs_to_esm_leaves_doom_style_dirname_module_alone: verifies
  that modules which declare their own __dirname (e.g. using
  fileURLToPath + dirname) are not double-shimmed
- maybe_cjs_to_esm_injects_dirname_without_filename_for_free_dirname:
  verifies that bare __dirname usage gets a dirname shim without
  forcing a __filename shim
- pijs_dynamic_import_loads_doom_style_wad_finder_module: end-to-end
  test verifying a module with self-declared __dirname loads and
  executes correctly through the full PiJs runtime, confirming the
  CJS shim does not interfere with user-provided dirname bindings

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
